identificationModule.briefTitle: Lifestyle Intervention for Glycemic Control in Type 2 Diabetes

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This study evaluates whether a structured diet and exercise intervention, delivered over 12 months, improves long-term blood glucose control compared to standard care in adults recently diagnosed with type 2 diabetes.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Inclusion Criteria: Adults aged 18-65; HbA1c between 7.0% and 10.0% at screening; diagnosed with type 2 diabetes within the past 5 years; able to participate in moderate physical activity. Exclusion Criteria: Type 1 diabetes; current insulin therapy; pregnancy; history of bariatric surgery; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Safety and Efficacy of a Novel GLP-1 Agonist in Type 2 Diabetes

descriptionModule.briefSummary: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial assessing the safety and glucose-lowering effect of a once-weekly injectable GLP-1 receptor agonist in adults with type 2 diabetes who are overweight or obese.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Inclusion Criteria: Adults 18 years or older; BMI of 27 or greater; HbA1c between 7.5% and 11.0%; on stable metformin therapy for at least 3 months. Exclusion Criteria: History of pancreatitis; personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma; severe gastrointestinal disease; current use of another GLP-1 agonist or insulin.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Continuous Glucose Monitoring in Pediatric Type 2 Diabetes

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This observational study examines whether continuous glucose monitoring devices improve glycemic awareness and outcomes in adolescents recently diagnosed with type 2 diabetes, compared to standard fingerstick monitoring.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Inclusion Criteria: Age 10-17 years; diagnosed with type 2 diabetes within the past 2 years; parent or guardian able to provide consent. Exclusion Criteria: Type 1 diabetes; current use of insulin pump; cognitive impairment preventing device use.

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This study tests whether weekly telehealth coaching sessions focused on diet, medication adherence, and self-monitoring improve HbA1c and quality of life in adults with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Inclusion Criteria: Adults 21 and older; HbA1c greater than 8.0% at enrollment; access to a smartphone or tablet with internet. Exclusion Criteria: Severe hearing or vision impairment preventing telehealth participation; current enrollment in another diabetes management program.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Gut Microbiome Changes Following Metformin Initiation

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This early-phase study characterizes how gut microbiome composition shifts in the first 12 weeks after starting metformin in newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes patients, to understand whether microbiome changes relate to drug response.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria: Inclusion Criteria: Adults 18-70; newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes, metformin-naive; willing to provide stool samples at 4 time points. Exclusion Criteria: Antibiotic use within the past 3 months; inflammatory bowel disease; prior bariatric surgery.
